qa_pairs.question: What are the RGU-core-memory bundle characteristics for Alliance high-performance computing systems?
qa_pairs.answer: On Alliance high-performance computing systems, RGU-core-memory bundle characteristics specify the ratio of cores and memory (in GB) associated with each Reference GPU Unit (RGU) for a particular cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific RGU-core-memory bundle ratios for the Fir and Narval clusters on Alliance systems?
qa_pairs.answer: For the Fir cluster, the RGU-core-memory bundle has 0.98 cores per RGU and 23.6 GB of memory per RGU. For the Narval cluster, the bundle has 3.00 cores per RGU and 31.1 GB of memory per RGU.
